title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat, but its widespread use could lead to potential risks if the tool contains unpatched vulnerabilities, is misconfigured, or is exploited by malicious actors. Additionally, reliance on automated tools may lead to overconfidence in security without proper validation.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough security review of the tool before deploying it in production environments.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool and apply them promptly to mitigate potential vulnerabilities.
Use the tool as part of a broader security strategy, ensuring manual validation of critical findings.

title: A vulnerability was found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this vulnerability is the function...
description: A vulnerability was found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this vulnerability is the function pcf_sess_sbi_discover_and_send of the component sm-policies Endpoint. Performing a manipulation results in denial of service. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS could lead to a denial of service (DoS) attack, potentially disrupting mobile network operations and services relying on the affected sm-policies Endpoint. Remote exploitation increases the risk of widespread impact.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply network-level mitigations, such as rate limiting or firewall rules, to restrict access to the vulnerable endpoint.
Monitor for unusual traffic patterns or signs of exploitation targeting the sm-policies Endpoint.
Engage with the Open5GS project maintainers to confirm a timeline for a patch and prepare to deploy updates as soon as they are available.

recommended_actions: Upgrade Open5GS to the latest version that addresses CVE-2026-8224, if available.
Implement network-level monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify and mitigate potential exploitation attempts.
Restrict access to the affected component (PCF) by enforcing strict access controls and firewall rules.

title: A vulnerability was identified in Industrial Application Software IAS Canias ERP 8.03. This issue...
description: A vulnerability was identified in Industrial Application Software IAS Canias ERP 8.03. This issue affects the function iasServerRemoteInterface.doAction of the component Java RMI Session Management. Such manipulation leads to improper authentication. The attack can be launched remotely. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in IAS Canias ERP 8.03 could allow unauthorized remote access due to improper authentication in Java RMI Session Management. This may lead to data breaches, unauthorized system control, or disruption of business operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately restrict access to the affected Java RMI interface by implementing network-level controls, such as firewalls or access control lists (ACLs).
Monitor for unusual activity on systems running IAS Canias ERP 8.03, and review logs for signs of exploitation attempts.
Engage with the vendor or community for potential patches or workarounds, and consider upgrading to a newer version if available.

Educate employees and IT staff about the vulnerability and ensure proper logging and auditing of ERP system activities.

title: Kdenlive before 26.04.1 allows dangerous proxy parameters when an attacker-controlled project...
description: Kdenlive before 26.04.1 allows dangerous proxy parameters when an attacker-controlled project file is used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Kdenlive before version 26.04.1 allows attackers to exploit dangerous proxy parameters via a maliciously crafted project file. This could potentially lead to arbitrary code execution or compromise of the user's system.
recommended_actions: Update Kdenlive to version 26.04.1 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Avoid opening untrusted or unknown project files in Kdenlive.
Implement endpoint protection solutions to detect and block malicious files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-45181.

title: GrapheneOS before 2026050400 allows attackers to discover the real IP address of a VPN user as a...
description: GrapheneOS before 2026050400 allows attackers to discover the real IP address of a VPN user as a consequence of a registerQuicConnectionClosePayload optimization, because an application can let system_server transmit UDP traffic on its behalf. This occurs when the "Block connections without VPN" and "Always-on VPN" settings are enabled.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows attackers to potentially identify the real IP address of a VPN user, undermining user privacy and anonymity. While the severity is marked as low, the issue could have significant implications for individuals relying on VPNs for security, such as journalists, activists, or users in restrictive environments.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of GrapheneOS (2026050400 or later) to patch the vulnerability.
Review and verify VPN settings, ensuring proper configuration of 'Block connections without VPN' and 'Always-on VPN' features.
Monitor for any unusual network activity that could indicate exploitation of this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-5337 - During the analysis, it was identified that authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access or
description: During the analysis, it was identified that authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access or higher are able to perform an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) attack. This vulnerability exists because the Frontend File Manager Plugin WordPress plugin through 23.6 does not properly validate user authorization for the requested uploaded file when processing download requests. By modifying the value of the 'file_id' parameter in the download endpoint (e.g., http://localhost/?do=wpfm_download&file_id=40&nm_file_nonce=a36fb893f1), an attacker can access files belonging to other users, including privileged users such as administrators. This allows unauthorized access/read to sensitive data stored  within the application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5337.

title: CVE-2026-5063 - The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cro
description: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via POST parameter key names in the submit_nex_form() function in versions up to, and including, 9.1.11 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. title: CVE-2026-40561 - Starlet versions through 0.31 for Perl allows HTTP Request Smuggling via Improper Header Precedence.
description:
Starlet versions through 0.31 for Perl allows HTTP Request Smuggling via Improper Header Precedence.

Starlet incorrectly prioritizes "Content-Length" over "Transfer-Encoding: chunked" when both headers are present in an HTTP request. Per RFC 7230 3.3.3, Transfer-Encoding must take precedence.

An attacker could exploit this to smuggle malicious HTTP requests via a front-end reverse proxy.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40561.
